source_pr.title: plan, statistics: plan topn more precisely.
source_pr.body:
For table t (a, b) which col a is pk, and b is index key, we were hard to choose IndexScan or TableScan.
Now we add a new dimension called `expectedCnt` for dynamic programming, which means the operator will be closed after returning `expectedCnt` records.
For SQL like `select * from t where b < 10 order by a limit 1`, if we choose to push the order to data source, the expected count will be 1, and the table scan expects to scan about `1/selectivity(b<10)`'s records. Index scan will sure to scan `Count * selectivity(b<10)`'s records. This will make cost more precise.

@winoros @lamxTyler PTAL

symptoms: sql: "select * from t where t.b <= 100 order by t.a limit 1", (plan/cbo_test.go)
failure_signature: sql: "select * from t where t.b <= 100 order by t.a limit 1", (plan/cbo_test.go)
